Javeria Hasnain’s 
SIN
 is a chapbook fluent in the privilege of pleasure and mortality. Quick-footed and capacious, this chapbook circles questions of morbidity, morality and mortality like a shark.

Javeria Hasnain is a poet, translator, and educator from Karachi. She is an MFA Poetry candidate and a Fulbright scholar at The New School. Her poems and prose have been published in several journals

1.snippet:
I desire, but desire itself. I enjoy 

the privilege of sitting

by the window when it rains, 

a lit Marlboro in hand, 

reading Ernaux or Milosz

or Blake. I pick 

Amma’s call, tell her it is not

Like that day, 

 when I remained still under you,

when you tried to pin your smallness into me. 

I don’t know the you I am referring to. 

Nevertheless, consider this an apology for being happy. Co

Mercy is a howl.

Mercy is how I

began. A coyote cries

in the botanical garden.

The animal’s limp 

glimpse reflecting my own

fears. One of which:

hunger. Each bone basking

in the belly’s faint
